text: You survived! You have it, then?
Let's hope it's as powerful as the Psijics believe it to be.
Return anytime. You're quite welcome here.
Remember, nothing but genuine fire salts will do... the forge knows the difference.
If you need any more smithing work, come see me again.
What brings you to Balimund today? Repair? Purchase?
Shor's blood, the Thu'um!
By the gods, is that the Voice?
Wow!
That must be the Voice!
That sound... it's deafening!
Could that be... the Voice?
Those words have such power!
That was amazing!
The Voice? Here?
That sound could bring mountains down!
That voice is power!
Louder than the roar of the Mane, that is!
S'rendarr's mercy!
Did anyone else hear that?
By the gods, my ears...
Now that's a real man!
Oh gods, my ears!
Now that's the true Voice!
What was that?
In the absence of the Moot, it would further legitimize his claim.
You don't stand a chance!
Now if you'll excuse me...
Bye.
I'm not allowed to stand on furniture.
Hands off.
You mind swinging that somewhere else?
Watch what you're doing.
Don't even think about it.
What can I do for you?
Hmm?
Good morning.
Amazing, eh? To be standing at the very entrance to Sky Haven Temple itself!
Are we close? Thalmor spies could be anywhere.
There have been whispers. A boy, up in Windhelm, name of Aventus Aretino. Been trying to contact... the Dark Brotherhood.
Lucan at the Riverwood Trader had his store broken into the other night. Oddly enough, barely anything's been stolen.
Nobody trusts those mages, way up north in their College. Gods only know what they do up there.
Maro is dead, I know. But we've got bigger problems right now!
The fool went absolutely berserk! He wounded Veezara, tried to kill me, and then he fled. I knew that lunatic couldn't be trusted.
Oh, but I will speak. I will speak to you. For you are the one.
Is that a threat? Back off, or by the Eight I'll cleave that arrogant head from its shoulders.
I'm sorry, but I really don't have time for this. Move along.
Keep back, citizen. I have important business I must attend to.
Agallon is an efficient man, and will certainly already have begun his tour of Skyrim. Unfortunately, we don't have his schedule.
So, you've got two options. Try to find Agallon yourself, somewhere on his route between the major cities. Or... find and steal his schedule.
You'll find that in his office, in the Legion stronghold of Dragon Bridge. It's your choice. Now go, and may Sithis speed you.
With the Emperor's arrival in Skyrim now a certainty, the Imperial Legion will need to begin its preparations immediately.
Security is being handled by a man named Voltus Agallon. His job is to travel throughout Skyrim, and ensure each city is prepared for Mede's visit.
You must find and kill Voltus Agallon. Doing so will disrupt security sufficiently that we will be able to, eventually, get to the Emperor.
What? Filthy assassin! We'll see who lies dead!
First of all, I need to sew confusion and doubt through the rebel troops. I want to assassinate their commanding officer.
And we have an opportunity to slay the rebel field commander. Without a clear command structure, they should fall into confusion and disarray.
This won't be easy, but that's why I'm sending you. Think you can handle it?
Your objective is Fort Sungard.
Your objective is Fort Snowhawk.
Your objective is Fort Greymoor.
Your objective is Fort Neugrad.
Your objective is Fort Dunstad.
Your objective is Fort Kastav.
Your objective is Fort Greenwall.
Your objective is the enemy held fort.
The rebels are keeping some of our men prisoner there. I aim to turn that into an advantage.
I've sent men to scout out the area. You are to meet them, find a way to slip in, free our men, and take over the fort.
They won't be expecting an attack from the inside.
Go in quiet, break our man out, then get out of there. Don't risk combat if you can avoid it. If you can't, then give 'em hell.
Yes, Papa.
Inside the house. Now.
Why? I want to watch the soldiers.
Your fealty is precious to me. I will make good use of it.
You bear my ring.
And skillfully too. I've been watching you for ages, it seems. You have the makings of a fine hunter.
You may even be my champion. Perhaps.
I am the spirit of the hunt, just one glimpse of the glorious stalker that your kind calls Hircine.
And found my favor. That skin will serve you well, child.
Look more closely at it. My glories shall protect you from all this world's grievances.
Good hunting.
You've done well, hunter.
There is no retribution in the hunt. It is not vengeance I seek, but the blood course of a living hunt.
There are others who would gladly accept my favor.
They will hunt him while you delay. Your choice.
Fly, my hunter. There are others who vie for my favor. A bit of competition. Don't dally while the prey flees.
The one who stole it has fled to what he thinks is his sanctuary.
Just as a bear climbs a tree to escape the hunt, but only ends up trapping himself.
Seek out this rogue shifter. Tear the skin from his body, and make it an offering to me.
I may consider it. But you must first do a service for my glory.
All right.
Thanks again.
Thanks! I thought I was going to rot in here. So what's the plan?
What's the plan?
Good! I've got a few scores to settle.
Amazing that such beauty could exist in a place as desolate as the volcanic tundra.
Praise Kynareth for allowing such beauty in the world.
A truly remarkable place, isn't it?
Hello there, friend. Have you come to enjoy the sights and sounds of this beautiful sanctuary, as I have? Truly remarkable, isn't it?
I wish I had more contracts to dole out. You handled those jobs like a born killer.
What? Raise the alarm!
Raise the alarm! We've been infiltrated!
You're gonna get me out of here?
You made it! I'm impressed. It was a gamble, but it turned out well.
Good. Glad we straightened that out.
I need to ask you to stop. That... shouting... is making people nervous.
You shouldn't leave weapons laying around. Someone could get hurt. And if I see you do it again, that someone will be you.
Have I made myself clear?
I've got my eye on you.
Can't say I've heard of any laws against... whatever that is you're doing. But I'll lock you up if I have to.
Tell me, Husband. Is it true what I've heard?
So, is it true?
Come now. What everyone else is saying. It can't possibly be true...
But really. A horker, some twine, three Wood Elves, and a hatchet? Points for creativity, if nothing else.
It's the part with the severed head I find most impressive. My compliments.
I'm just trying to imagine you hiding inside the body of a mammoth. Bloody brilliant idea.
Ah, you must mean my little adventure on the ship...
Oh, you mean in that manor house? During the thunderstorm? Heh. What can I say? It happened, just like you heard.
It's all thanks to a special technique I developed. I call it the "Razor-Winged Butterfly." I'd teach it to you... but then I'd have to kill you.
Romantic, isn't? Like that time we first met. Remember that? Never seen so much blood. Ah, to be young again...
Daanik Kendov!
One of the quickest ways to make some coin in Skyrim is to clear out some of the wealthier homes of their valuables.
If you're interested, I could point out a good place that could use a little housecleaning.
What do you say?
I think we have a nice wealthy home for you to break into. Think you can clear the place of valuables and keep it quiet?
These people just inherited some heirlooms from a relative. Good timing for us, bad timing for them, eh?
Just got a tip on this house... should be stuffed with loot. Care to head over there and find out?
Got a message from one of my people that this place just came into some coin. Should be a good target. Want to take it?
Someone with connections to our Guild wants us to hit this place and send a message by clearing it of valuables. You want to handle it?
Good timing. The people in this home recently got one of your fellow Guild mates sent to jail. How about you make them pay for it?
You can give this house a once over. I hear they're fairly well-off and probably have lots of good stuff ripe for the picking. Sound good?
The owner of this place just got an inheritance from a relative lost in the war. Sound like something you can sink your teeth into?
You've been here for a while, right? What was it like back when we could still make runs out to the coast?
The Captain has it locked away in that drowned ship by his quarters. He says it's "to keep it safe" but I know he's just keeping it for himself.
Maybe. For now just lay low and do your job. I have a feeling we'll be rid of the Captain soon...
Well, before that cave-in sealed our ships in here we could loot any size ship we wanted.
If only we could get our hands on that treasure. We could get out of here and never look back.
Where did you keep all that loot?  I've been here almost a year and haven't seen any of it.
I thank you for your kindness.
I am a traveler. A pilgrim. I follow the voice of Kynareth wherever it can be heard.
I've dreamed of seeing Eldergleam for years. Might I travel alongside you? I promise not to get in the way.
I understand. Some journeys must be taken alone. I'll be here if you change your mind.
Follow me. I think I can convince the tree to help us.
Well... yes. There is something.
It won't repair the tree back at the temple, but we could bring them a new one.
You would violate this marvel of Kynareth's glory to fix that half-breed stump in Whiterun?
That's abominable. Barbaric. I'll have no part of this. Why didn't you tell me what you intended?
I had no idea you were a man of violence. What exactly are your intentions in this blessed place?
With pleasure. Nature has a way of finding vengeance, though. You walk a bleak path, friend. One that will lead to suffering.
Very well. I can't stop you. But I can't bear to watch it, either.
Ah, well, I recently approached Sibbi Black-Briar about buying Frost, one of the finest breeding horses in Skyrim. He agreed to sell.
I paid Sibbi half the cost of Frost up front, but before he could deliver, Maven had him locked up. Sibbi believes this exempts him from our deal.
It does not.
I want you to talk to Sibbi for me. Then, I want you to bring me Frost and the lineage papers that prove the horse's worth.
Ah, that's a real shame. You would've fit the bill nicely.
Good man. You won't regret it.
Don't tell me - Sibbi and Louis Letrush? Well, Sibbi is in jail and Letrush is certainly welcome to try. My hired help is more than a match for him.
Looking for work? I need someone to deliver a message to Sibbi Black-Briar.
Go on, then. Deliver my message to Sibbi.
Am I going to have to beat it out of you? Is that what you want?
You start flapping that jaw of yours or I'll start flapping it for you. You understand me?
Where'd you stash it?
(Sigh) Let's just kill them both, eh?
(Whimpering)
(Sobbing)
Honest, I didn't take it!
Look at me when I'm talking to you, eh?
I... I don't know, it wasn't me. I swear!
What do you need, guardian?
Yes, champion of Azura?
The Twilight watches us all, guardian.
Sad, isn't it? We can't afford to keep the place running since these attacks started.
Pirates, you see.
Raiding all along the coast, from Hammerfell to Vvardenfell. Only the Shatter-Shields appear safe.
Just in time. This is Adelaisa, one of my superiors from the Company.
She's been dispatched to... eh... see to it that we get back on schedule.
You should probably speak to her.
Ha! Heard of 'em? I've been captaining a crew of 'em for well on nine years now. What's it to you?
I can't express how much easier you've made my job.
Maybe Adelaisa will finally ease up, but I expect she won't.
Ah, yes, your pay. The Company thanks you for your services. Should we ever be able to help you, stop by.
How did you get it? No, never mind, I probably shouldn't know.
I see... it looks like Suvaris has been traveling to Dawnstar to meet with a pirate crew there.
Get to Dawnstar and see what you can find out about these rogues. I'd wager they won't be too far from the tavern.
Try to find out where their fortress is. The captain, Stig Salt-Plank, has to know something.
Ha, I knew you'd join up! Great news. We'll take back Skyrim together, eh?
I'm glad I'll have you beside me in there. This place chills my blood, and I don't care who knows it.
Well done, indeed. This will be of great service. Understanding the enemy's position makes us all the more equipped to usurp it.
And with the rebels thinking us occupied elsewhere for the time being, we'll be able to take them by surprise.
Well, now that you mention it, there is something I've been trying to get my hands on.
Great Malacath, we beseech you, aid us in our time of need...
How went your meeting with Ennodius Papius? Did you prove his paranoia legitimate?
I see you're back from Dawnstar. Beitild lies dead, I hope?
You're alive. Then I guess you haven't paid a visit to the vampire yet, hmm?
Please tell me you flayed that horrible bard alive and made a drum from his skin...
Taken care of that layabout Deekus, yet? Or does the idea of killing a fellow Argonian make your scales crawl?
No new boots? Does this mean the Argonian is still alive?
You smell like dead cat. Can I assume Ma'randru-jo has been taken care of?
How went your encounter with Ma'randru-jo? Back in Hammerfell, I always loved watching two cats rip each other to shreds...
Hmph. No arrows protruding from your skull... Can I assume Anoriath is dead, then?
Ah, back from Fort Greymoor. The old servant has been put out of her misery, I hope?
So, you've returned from Mzinchaleft! A dwarven ruin... what an exotic place for a kill. Assuming Maluril is actually... dead?
Back from your little vacation in Falkreath. So, did you enjoy the sights, sample the food... kill the housecarl?
You haven't faced that pirate captain yet, have you? Safia? I only ask because you're still alive...
So many contracts, so little time...
I don't have anything for you just yet. Please, talk to Nazir, and see what he has available.
I'm still trying to find a suitable contract for you. Continue to work with Nazir on the, um, supplemental assignments.
The Keeper will be arriving soon, with the body of the Night Mother. So many preparations to be made...
Whatever it is those Psijics are up to. They cannot be trusted.
For all we know, he's here to scout out the College. I'm sure they want to undermine our efforts.
This! This... Orb. The Eye of Magnus, whatever you want to call it. It's clearly powerful, and no doubt they want it for themselves!
I expect to be informed if they make any future attempts to contact you.
Well? What is the meaning of this?
I'm not sure what just happened, but I assure you I will get to the bottom of it.
What? What trickery is this? You're not going anywhere until I find out what you're up to!
Don't play coy. You asked to see a specific member of the College. Here he is. Now what is it that you want?
Don't play coy. You asked to see a specific member of the College. Here she is. Now what is it that you want?
There's been a misunderstanding. Clearly I should not be here. I shall simply take my leave.
I'm sorry, I'm afraid I don't understand.
I am not "up to" anything. I apologize if I have offended you in any way.
I can see to that. We'll need plenty of fresh corpses to rebuild her army, you see.
None can stand against the Wolf Queen!
You've come far, mortal. No doubt you seek to enter Potema's Sanctum.
'Accidents' like this don't just happen! Someone is trying to sabotage my uncle's research!
Did she, now? Well, as it turns out, there are a few lingering contracts we haven't had the chance to complete just yet.
And more, dribbling in from time to time. I'll assign them to you as they become available. To be completed at your leisure.
Ah, you're a smart woman. That's exactly what I wanted to hear.
Head to the Riften jail and talk to Sibbi. Get him to tell you where Frost and his lineage papers are and bring them to me outside Riften.
You'll be well paid for your time.
But this is supposed to be your work.
I really don't have time to deal with you right now. Please just let me get back to my work.
You ready, Auxiliary?
Of course you are! I prayed, and you came, and now you'll accept my contract!
My mother, she... she died. I... I'm all alone now. So they sent me to that terrible orphanage in Riften. Honorhall.
The headmistress is an evil, cruel woman. They call her Grelod the Kind. But she's not kind. She's terrible. To all of us.
So I ran away, and came home. And performed the Black Sacrament. Now you're here! And you can kill Grelod the Kind!
Aretino? Why that little bastard! You tell him I'm coming for him! And when I find him, it'll be the beating of his miserable life!
You don't have to say anything. There's no need. You're here, so I know you'll accept my contract.
It took so long. So very long. But now that you're here, you can accept my contract.
The what? Why how dare you! I will not be intimidated in my own orphanage! Get out! Get out this instant!
What are you staring at, you worthless piece of gutter trash? I simply must start locking the doors again...
I have a family heirloom you can have. Supposed to be sort of valuable. I hope that's all right.
It worked! I knew you'd come, I just knew it!
I did the Black Sacrament, over and over. With the body, and the... things. And then you came! An assassin from the Dark Brotherhood!
Finally! My prayers have been answered!
I had no idea you were a woman of violence. What exactly are you intending to do here?
I look forward to it.
Shhh!
After I've finished showing you the grounds, we can talk.
How do you think we should spend the remainder of our classroom time?
If you have any further questions, can you please hold them until I've finished?
Likewise. Now, I have a lot to do, so I'm afraid you'll just have to leave!
We've run into a rough patch lately, but it's nothing to be concerned about.
Tell you what. You keep making us coin and I'll worry about everything else. Fair enough?
What's on your mind?
Petty threats and fist waving are not going to sway me. You people are all talk, and everyone knows it!
You demand payment for protection and you can't even protect yourselves!
Look, I can't make the coin appear out of thin air. Please, be reasonable. I'll... I'll pay next month.
Despite your mistakes, I see more potential in you than half the snot-nosed footpads that stumble their way into the Flagon.
I'm going to put my neck on the line. We need people like you in our outfit.
You... you have my statue! What are you going to do with it?
And so have I. What's the point of paying anyway? Your outfit can't even fend for itself. I could do better tossing the gold into the sewer.
You can't scare me with your tough talk. I'm not paying you people a single coin.
I hope you're here for friendlier reasons now... after all, I'm all paid up.
No! That urn was priceless!
Please, reconsider what you're doing. Maybe I can pay later...
I... I don't care! I'm not paying!
I'm sorry, you're going to have to leave. Tell Brynjolf he'll just have to make do without my coin.
So, can I interest you in anything today?
Look, I got the message. What else did you want?
Please! Don't take the statue! It's the only thing of value I have left!
Ask all you want, you can't squeeze blood from a stone. Not in this part of the world anyway.
It's you! Please, don't hurt me!
What did you want?
Then I thank you for showing me mercy. Here, take this coin to him. If it keeps us together, it's worth its weight.
Don't fool yourself. It's only a matter of time before you people are run out of Riften.
I surrender! I surrender!
Every time I think of you, I imagine a saber cat closing its claws around your face.
It's so good to see you again.
You're mean. I hate you. I hate you forever and ever and ever.
I hope monsters come and eat you.
I bet you could slay one of those mean old dragons. I bet you could do anything.
You're the best! Can you be my father?
You're the best! Can you be my mother?
First off, they've got some rebels holed up in a nearby settlement. Need to clean them out so our boys can move in.
First, we need to limit Stormcloak capacity and send them a not so subtle message by sabotaging a resource they claim to control.
First, we have information regarding the movements of a rebel courier. We need to intercept him and confiscate his package.
And perhaps more importantly, I need to break one of our boys out of a rebel controlled fort.
Secondly, we got more of 'em hiding in a village, using the local populace as shields. Not to worry. We can dispatch them without harming the locals.
Secondly, I need to cripple rebel production capabilities with a little sabotage.
Soldiers win wars, but where to put them is the key. We have word of an enemy courier in the area. We aim to confiscate his documents.
What's that?
Very well. You, outsider, come here! I want a word.
Now, we begin the ritual...
So it will be. Malacath has spoken, Yamarz. Your path is clear.
This is all your fault, you know.
I'm stuck fighting a giant now, thanks to you. So you're going to help me.
You're going with me, and you're going to make sure I don't have any trouble reaching that giant.
Don't worry, I'll make it worth your while.
Let's get this over with.
If there's a chance this will save me, then I'm taking it.
Excellent. Now, you must come with me. You've become a part of this. You must be present for the ritual.
You don't deserve to call yourself an Orc! You're weak, you're small, and you're an embarrassment!
What? What is this?
Why are we bothering with this?
I'll lead the way, but you'd better back me up.
You dare summon me, Yamarz?
This giant... It's not the only thing in Fallowstone Cave. Getting to it isn't going to be easy.
You're going to clear a path to the giant, so I can conserve my strength. I'll make sure you're paid for it.
So you meet me at Fallowstone Cave, and I'll get this over with. I can have my tribe back, and you can... You can leave us alone.
You pathetic weakling!
Thanks to you, I've just been issued a challenge in front of my whole tribe, by a daedric prince, no less. I can't ignore that.
I didn't say I wouldn't fight the giant. I just said you're going to help me get to it.
Meet me outside Fallowstone Cave. You clear the way to the giant for me, and I'll make sure you get paid for it.
You let giants... Giants!... overrun my shrine. Bring me their leader's club as an offering, and I might release you from this curse!
Yes, you. You, who shares my iron tomb, who warms my ancient bones. I give you this task - journey to Volunruud. Speak with Amaund Motierre.
What? What treachery! Defiler! Debaser and defiler! You have violated the sanctity of the Night Mother's coffin! Explain yourself!
Speak, worm!
She... she said that? She said those words... to you? "Darkness rises when silence dies"?
But those are the words. The Binding Words. Written in the Keeping Tomes. The signal so I would know. Mother's only way of talking to sweet Cicero...
Then... it's true! She's back! Our lady is back! She has chosen a Listener! She has chosen you! Ha ha ha! All hail the Listener!
Oh, but how can I defend you? How can I exert your will if you will not speak? To anyone!
She... spoke to you? More treachery! More trickery and deceit! You lie! The Night Mother speaks only to the Listener!
And there is... no... Listener!
What do you want, milk drinker?
The gods know what you've done.
A fine day to you, friend. May you die with a sword in your hands.
May the gods watch over your battles, friend.
Oh, it's you. I was wondering why I was smelling something unpleasant.
Absolutely no time to deal with lowlifes these days. Go away.
So nice to have pleasant company for a change.
There are so few pleasures in life as fine as your company.
Filth. Run to the horizon before I hunt you down.
There are so many delightful ways I've pictured you dead.
Azura's wisdom to you, friend.
We're one of the same kind, you and I. I'm glad to have met you.
For your crimes, may Malacath boil your blood and grind your bones.
What do you want? Aren't your hands coated with enough blood?
I ain't afraid of the old hag. Still, I wish Constance was in charge. She treats us real good.
Constance, sometimes she gives me little snacks. Which is good, 'cause we only get but one meal. Late, too, in the afternoon.
I'm a growing boy. I need as much food as I can get.
Miss Grelod is... well she's a terrible old crone. No person could be that cruel. I think she's part hagraven.
She only lets us out in the yard once. In the morning. And she just stands there watching us.
Like I said. Hagraven.
I hate her. Everyone hates her. She's the meanest person I ever met. Sometimes she even locks us in the room.
Constance tries to stop her, and is real good to us, but it's never enough.
Sometimes, when she goes to her room to work or sleep, I slip out for a bit.
A boy's gotta make a livin', right?
I will do nothing of the sort. You have no business here. I'm sorry, but you really must leave.
May your next fight bring you victory, friend.
May your weapons be sharp and your prey fresh.
Its scent is foul. Unclean. Hurtful.
Khajiit wishes for you to go away. You have done us too much harm.
These sands are cold, but Khajiit feels warmness from your presence.
Speak. Khajiit will listen until all the sugars in the world turn sour.
It fills us with rage. What harm does it bring to us now?
It annoys us. What does it want to take from us now?
Ours is to smile at your passing, friend.
It is our most favored company.
Please take the Lexicon.
Haven't I always been a good father? A good chief?
Yes, wouldn't want to miss the lush summer we have ahead.
There you go, Son. Be your own man!
You just need to remember that there's a war on, and firewood isn't cheap these days.
Do you ever get a weird feeling about those sisters?
My eyes are sharp.
That's just it... if she's not happy, there are plenty of other women who will be.
Don't you ever wonder, Father?
Come on, we just need a little elbow grease.
Why else have they cursed me? Killed all my wives?
Oh, good point. I wish my head were made of rocks.
It's good to toughen her up. Not an easy world out here. Keep at it.
But we choose our own path now.
How will I know when I'm ready?
Oh, like the sleepy rebel supporters in this town are going to tell anyone.
Oh, really?
They're miners. Harmless.
But I thought there was only one chief.
I've just never heard of a midwife losing all her mothers like that.
Mama, when can we go play in the river again?
They're fearsome!
Would you look at that!
What are you on about? Let's just get the treasure here and go.
Don't rush ahead. This is a trap if I ever saw one.
This is it! Gathrik awaits us!
Well, we have to cut down something!
Oh, if you're worried about me trying to meddle with you...
What about my father? Was he a good hunter?
"Next season for sure..." What? Uncle Calcelmo, I don't understand what you mean.
Yes, Thongvor. I'll draft the letter immediately.
How are your chores coming, Son?
Whatever. Just get back to work. Now.
All your drinks are watered down, Kleppr. You aren't fooling me, and you know Lisbet can't afford to pay me enough for the finest drinks anyhow.
What do you need?
Our son joining the Stormcloaks. If this war had never happened, Cade would have joined the Imperial Legion, like Grandfather.
Father, why do you and Mother fight so much?
I have some thoughts. There are three crucial elements. Some may be easier to find than others.
On the top of the Throat of the World is a patch of unmelting snow. No heat can touch it.
Then we need the tusk of a mammoth, ground to a fine powder as only the giants know how.
The final step is tricky. It requires the briar heart from a Forsworn of The Reach.
If you can bring me these materials, the Phial can again be made whole.
You've shown yourself useful so far. All these lunks are too afraid of Haldyn to head in before the fog's cleared.
So if you're willing to perform one more service for the Company, we could put this matter behind us for good.
Take out Haldyn, and we'll handle the rest.
Good to hear. You can be a difficult man to find. Thankfully those couriers are tenacious.
Good to hear. You can be a difficult woman to find. Thankfully those couriers are tenacious.
What do you say?
Hmph.
Voada, you spilled laundry water in the soup pot again! Do I need to cut off your fingers to teach you a lesson?
Why are you still standing around here? Go get me the Phial, you fool.
Who do you think brought Septimus here? Who do you think protected you on your journey to open the box and loose my knowledge on this world?
Your free will is an illusion. Whether you acknowledge me or not is your own business. But I will be in your mind.
Ooooh, an observant one. How clever to ask of Septimus.
This Dwemer lockbox. Look upon it and wonder.
Inside is the heart. The heart of a god! The heart of you. And me.
But it was hidden away. Not by the dwarves, you see. They were already gone.
Someone else. Unseen. Unknown. Found the heart, and with a flair for the ironical, used dwarven trickery to lock it away.
When the top level was built, no more could be placed. It was and is the maximal apex.
How long will it be sung? My feet were set upon the rock but it turned to mud and drew me down.
It lick the panes and smokes the glass...
Yes, you're innocent. As innocent as anyone can be in this city.
I guess we also owe you something for locking you up. Here, my family's ring. And everything the guards confiscated from you.
Now if you'll excuse me, I have a mine that needs new workers to fill it.
My eyes inside Cidhna Mine tell me that Madanach is dead.
You've done a great service to the Silver-Blood family. I've had the jarl officially pardon you, and take care of a few other loose ends.
The Dark Brotherhood? Yeah, yeah, yeah. You think this is the first contract I've had on my life?
I'm a pirate, sweetie. Assassination attempts are an occupational hazard. Now, shall we?
And why would you be looking for me, hmm? I am not sure I appreciate your presence here. Friend.
Death? Oh, there's death to be found here, to be sure.
But not my own!
Ah, I see. The old Dark Brotherhood "silent intimidation" treatment.
Oh, I know who you are. Don't be so surprised. You ain't the first assassin sent to kill me. Won't be the last. Now let's get this over with.
Go away! I don't know who you are, but this is my spot! Whatever's on that ship is mine!
Who are you? Why are you here? Leave this place. I have much work to conduct...
Yes... quite. But if that power could be harnessed. Controlled. The possibilities would be...
But... who exactly are you? And why are you in here? I think it best you leave Mzinchaleft. These ruins can be quite... dangerous.
You'll have to catch me first!
Sithis? Let me guess: "In the Void!" By the gods, you Dark Brotherhood are a predictable lot, aren't you?
Okay, here's how this works. You try to assassinate me, and I kill you. Horribly. I have done this dance before, you know.
What? Why? I don't understand... I've done nothing to wrong you. Please, mercy!
What? You dare challenge Ma'randru-jo? You will suffer for your insolence!
Fiend! You will not leave here alive! Guards! Guards!
Now aren't you a strange one. Well, if you're here to shop, shop. If not, find some other caravan to stalk.
I've been expecting you.
Should have figured the little lunatic would completely snap.
Still here? What's the matter, can't handle what I'm throwing your way?
And why would I... Oh. Oh! Guards! Guards, I need assistance!
Right. Well. If you have some kind of official business with the jarl, I'm afraid now is not the time. Go on then. Off with you.
Onmund, please search that area over there. See if you notice any... residual energies. Alive or undead.
Now, let's see. What shall we have the rest of you do?
J'zargo... what shall we have you do... Ah! Why don't you verify that we're the first ones here? Look for any amount of tampering with the tombs.
Brelyna, my dear, why don't you search for warding magics. Anything designed to keep people out. Don't interact with them, just identify them.
Well, are there any questions before we begin?
And here we all are. Shall we step inside?
All right, everyone. Let's be careful, but have fun!
Ah, yes. Hmm. Well, why don't you see if you can assist Arniel Gane? He's one of our scholars, here working on cataloging our finds.
I expect he'd appreciate some help in locating any additional magical artifacts here in the ruins.
Any enchanted items will do; the usefulness of the enchantment is irrelevant. If you find anything, the class can look it over.
Find Arniel Gane. He's somewhere in the northern section of the ruins. He'll show you what to do.
No? I daresay we don't have all day.
Still? If I have to wait much longer, you may need to just bury me here.
Help! Get that beast out of my house!
It's still here! Get it out!
Get that thing out of here!
Thank you! I thought that beast would be the end of me!
What about them? They seem loyal enough.
No, no, girl. It's the pickled spider tongue and then the decayed taproot. Do it the other way, and your cure turns into a poison.
Me? I like to talk to my targets before the kill. Taunt them, scare them... even challenge them.
That can have interesting results. Some victims may flee, others may cower in fear.
If you can goad a target into attacking you first, you may even find any local guards coming to your aid.
Just be yourself. Eliminate the targets any way you see fit. Quiet, loud, quick, slow - whatever you feel is best.
Occasionally I'll give you a contract with certain parameters. Stay within them, and you'll earn yourself a bonus.
The emissary? What's this about?
Wait... who are you? Intruder!
Ha. I'd like to see those arrogant bastards taken down a notch. Always looking down their noses at us lowly footsloggers.
If a dragon does show up, maybe we'll get lucky and it will eat the mages first. Might give us enough time to kill it.
You gave those mongrels a real whipping, eh? Good work. We'll be sure to send a garrison to reinforce the area and keep the peace.
We hit 'em where it hurts. As soon as we can, we'll take over - make the needed repairs. In the meantime, the people no longer slave for the Empire.
Good work. Knowing more about the enemy's position makes it that much easier to take it from him.
And of course, they'll be caught off guard thinking we're off doing something altogether different.
Ah, good. I've been wondering how we were supposed to defend this place from a dragon.
No. They're high mages, just in from Alinor. I guess herself is finally getting worried about all the dragon attacks.
Did you see those robes march in this morning? Who are they with? More of the emissary's treaty enforcers?
Not a day goes by I don't ask that question myself, my dear.
Don't be. We all make mistakes.
Give us a coin, Cosnach. You'll just spend it on cheap ale, anyway.
Ah, Yngvar, here to sing? You know an employee of the Silver-Blood family is always a guest of honor here.
Reburrus, I need you to draft a letter to Ulfric requesting more men.
I miss those days, before the Forsworn took up arms again, when you could walk the roads in peace.
Forgive me for worrying about the future of Markarth. The lifeblood of a city is gold, Igmund, and it doesn't stop bleeding because there's a war on.
Are you sure?
I... I'm sorry. I just, well, the forge gets so hot and I thought that...
Mmmhmm.
How did you manage to cripple yourself, Degaine?
Oh, Talos' breath, Reburrus. Skip ahead to the good part.
What can I get for you, sir?
What can I get for you, ma'am?
Yes? What do you need?
No... Not again!
Do your worst, monster!
I won't bend. Never!
Right away, sir!
I don't know, my darling wife. I assume you're going to tell me?
I don't buy that story, either.
And what do Legion soldiers know about the hills of The Reach and the caves that make up those hills? The Forsworn can hide and ambush every assault.
Shouldn't you be helping me then, wife of mine? Sharing my problems? Being my better half?
We sold all those lovely little necklaces you brought me today. Did you make them yourself?
I see. Well, let's continue this discussion later, then.
Endon, we talked about this... Are you still disappointed in Cade's choice?
Bothela, I have a question...
Ready to pay the toll? One shiv.
I'll let that comment go because you're new here. Ask things out of turn again, and we have a problem. Now, are we clear on all of this?
Then find one. That dung heap Grisvar's been known to make a few.
This better not be another waste of the Guild's resources, Brynjolf.
Good. Then I think it's time we put your expertise to the test.
If you're not sure maybe you don't belong here. I'll ask again, are we clear on all of this?
Goldenglow Estate brought in a mountain of gold for the Guild. You could almost call it our sweetest deal.
Then out of the clear blue, Aringoth stops sending us our cut. Mercer was... well, angry to put it kindly.
So we send in Vex and find out he's hired a bunch of mercenaries to guard the place.
The catch is that you can't burn the whole place to the ground.
That important client Mercer mentioned would be furious if you did.
Mercer? This is the one I was talking about... our new recruit.
You've made a mess of things and Maven's furious. I told you not to burn more than three of the hives!
I've smoothed things over with her for now, but you can forget your cut.
Ah, now that's a good idea. Head on in. But don't try anything in there. Madanach is smarter than you think.
They're built like small fortresses to resist the weather, but their one weakness is flame.
Besides, nothing tells the people of Riften we mean business better than a huge column of smoke.
Maven prefers that Aringoth remains alive, but if he tries to stop you from getting the job done, kill him.
The Guild has a lot riding on this. Don't make me look foolish by mucking it up.
That's not how things work around here. The Guild depends on an arrangement of influential people to keep things running smoothly.
Goldenglow Estate is a bee farm; they raise the wretched little things for honey. It's owned by some smart-mouth Wood Elf named Aringoth.
We need you to teach him a lesson by burning down three of the estate's hives and clearing out the safe in the main house.
And with no strings attached. Not bad. Here, got some coin for you.
Amazing what people leave laying around for us to take, isn't it?
Fine. I guess you'll need your coin then.
Here's your gold. Try not to spend it all in one place.
Are you out of your mind? We said no bloodshed! Well, guess what. You can just forget about your gold.
I thought it was crystal clear... no one dies on these jobs. Remember that next time you want to get paid.
You an assassin or a thief? Next time, keep your blade sheathed if you want to make any gold.
You drew blood, so you aren't getting paid. That's the rules. You don't like it, take it up with Brynjolf.
You idiot! We have to cover your mess up and that costs us gold... which means you don't get any.
I need some more supplies. Can you help out with that?
Some other time? That's not going to help me much, now is it?
If you're feeling adventurous, I'm always looking for someone to procure valuable books from some more... dangerous locations.
If you can bring me three more, that would be helpful.
Well all right. Here, I scribbled down the last place I'd heard about. Check it out, but be careful.
Oh, yes. Thank you. I'll get to work on this right away.
Sure is. I've got another client -- wants me to do some enchanting. You can do the pick-up for me, if you want.
In fact, there is. I need a soul gem filled, if you're up to it.
Ah. Some other time, perhaps?
Excellent. Thank you, I'll see that this gets put to good use.
Yes, a Grand one. Soon, please.
Here, take this Grand Soul Gem, fill it for me, and bring it right back. I'll be waiting.
Yes, yes I did. I've already sent instructions, you just need to make sure this arrives in one piece.
I'm running low on Grand Soul Gems.
All right. Be quick about it, will you? This is a good way for the College to bring in some money.
Here's the name and location of the client.
No! Not again! I shall not be denied again!
You must be the one Falk spoke so highly of. Welcome.
I'll admit, even I'm having a hard time disagreeing with you...
What are you waiting for? My husband is out there somewhere with that lunatic. Find them! Do what you've been tasked to do!
Hush, Veezara. You were very brave. Astrid may well be dead if not for you.
Look, we've got to deal with this situation. You've got to deal with this situation.
Achh... Thank you, dear. You are most kind. The jester's cut feels as bad as it looks, I'm afraid.
It's all up to you now... Listener.
Bested by a fool. Who's the fool now, hmm?
She's right. I'll be forever in your debt, dearest brother. Now be quiet. Just... just rest.
I want you to find that miserable little fool and end his life!
But first... find my husband. Make sure he's all right.
After the attack, Arnbjorn flew into a rage. When Cicero left... Arnbjorn went after him. They disappeared into the wild.
Search Cicero's room. Maybe there's something in there that sheds some light on where he might have gone.
Let me know the minute you find something. I've got to see to Veezara, and calm everyone down.
Dammit, this never should have happened! We knew better. We knew better, and still we let our guards down. Agh!
Just try to relax, Veezara. Let the elixir do its work. You'll feel better, shortly.
Move along, citizen.
I know that he still worships Talos. That he hates the Thalmor almost as much as the Stormcloaks do.
That he worries about being chased from Whiterun.
That he... that I'm...  that I don't have the same mother as my brother and sister.
Regrettably, I cannot reach your plane so directly.
But I forgive you for not knowing who I am. Few hear my whispers anymore.
I am Mephala, the Lady of Whispers.
I tug at the web of connections between mortals. Love, hatred, loyalty, betrayal.
The boy was good at sussing out secrets. You, I expect to take a more active role.
The castle is old. Lots of places nobody's been in a long while.
Places where you can overhear things. See things.
And the Whispering Lady.
She won't tell me her name.
I've gotten good at listening to keyholes. At the door in the basement, I hear her talking to me.
I thought I was caught, but she started telling me even more secrets.
But I can't open the door.
In the basement. Trust me, you'll see it.
I bet she'll talk to you too.
First, you must open this door.
A piece of my power has been locked away behind it, and even my eyes cannot see past the seals.
I'd much rather it be in the hands of an ambitious and talented person such as yourself.
What do you think? Are we going to let Ulfric dictate terms to us before the negotiations even start?
By Ysmir's beard, the nerve of those Imperial bastards, eh?
To think that we would sit down with that... Thalmor bitch. I say she walks or we walk.
Hagraven or horker, it matters not. We've got the arrows to fell any game.
So what can I get you?
Look at you! Betcha think you're somethin', huh?
All right, you convinced me. Doubt I'd be much good to you, anyway. The little fop cut me pretty deep.
But I slashed him good. Pretty sure I severed an artery. Don't know what you're going to find in there... but you can probably just follow the blood.
Oh, it's ancient. Apparently one of the first Sanctuaries in Skyrim. It hasn't been used by the Brotherhood in close to a hundred years.
If you say Cicero has gone there, it stands to reason that he's familiar with the place. That means you'll be fighting him on his own ground.
Tread carefully.
In there! Through the door. Some old Sanctuary, by the looks of it. I would have followed him, but I don't know the phrase.
Good hunting. I'll see you back at the Sanctuary.
Should have figured Astrid would send you.
Look, the Dark Brotherhood is a family. This Sanctuary is a family. And, we've always welcomed those... shunned by society.
Werewolves, wizards, eternal ten-year-old vampires... what does it matter? In truth, I've rarely met a lunatic I haven't liked.
Cicero's problem isn't his madness. It's an adherence to an ancient, outmoded way of life. The Night Mother's ways... simply are not our ways.
He just couldn't accept that. And now he'll have to pay the price.
What gave it away?
Ha ha ha ha ha.
Yeah, got to admit that little jester is good with that butter knife. But don't worry, I gave as good as I got.
Good. I'm spread thin. I could use the help.
We're taking the city. Meet the brothers gathering for the attack. Fight well or die well. Talos be with you.
Good. If you need any details on your marks, I'll be here. Now get going.
She began to spend a lot of time with Alva. Yet just a week before, she despised her.
In fact, the night she disappeared, she was supposed to meet Alva.
Alva told me later that she never showed up. I never got to tell her goodbye.
You found me! Laelette was trying to find me, too, but I'm glad you found me first.
Laelette was told to burn mommy and me, but she didn't want to.
She wanted to play with me forever and ever.
She kissed me on the neck, and I got so cold that the fire didn't even hurt.
Laelette thought she could take me and keep me, but she can't. I'm all burned up.
Now there's a mystery.
She ran away when the war started. Her husband, Thonnir, was devastated. No note or nothin'.
I'm pretty sure she up and joined the Stormcloaks.
Laelette? I thought she left to join the Stormcloaks. Ah! My poor Laelette!
Very well. Here. Take this back to Brynjolf and tell him he'll have no more trouble from me.
My wife died. Things won't be the same without her.
My husband died. Things won't be the same without him.
My father died. Things won't be the same without him.
My mother died. Things won't be the same without her.
My son died. Things won't be the same without him.
My sister died. Things won't be the same without her.
My brother died. Things won't be the same without him.
My aunt died. Things won't be the same without her.
My uncle died. Things won't be the same without him.
My niece died. Things won't be the same without her.
My nephew died. Things won't be the same without him.
My cousin died. Things won't be the same without her.
My cousin died. Things won't be the same without him.
My kinsman died. Things won't be the same without him.
My kinswoman died. Things won't be the same without her.
My friend died. Things won't be the same without her.
My friend died. Things won't be the same without him.
My girlfriend died. Things won't be the same without her.
My boyfriend died. Things won't be the same without him.
All right! I get it. I'll pay on time from now on. Just don't smash anything else.
Here, take your gold and leave me in peace.
Not Lady Dibella? No, please! I can't lose her!
I get the message. Here, take your gold. I hope you choke on it!
How I envy you. I can only imagine the excitement of seeing history through the eyes of another!
Sadly, I am resigned to just reading of its wonders through my research of the Skull.
It... it worked. Mara be praised!
You vanished after drinking the Torpor and materialized on the other side. I have never seen anything quite like it.
I thought I was clear regarding the dangers that awaited you within the Dreamstride.
However, this hardly matters any longer as you appear to have exited unscathed.
After what you just experienced, you are not amazed? I am at a loss for words.
We used to think the pirates had an army of battlemages at their side.
As it turns out, it was just one. Haldyn. I've since learned more about him.
The most dangerous sort of man -- quick to anger, but patient in exacting vengeance.
He also has a fearsome control of Destruction magic.
I wish you the best of luck.
A very powerful family in Windhelm.
They run a private shipping operation, and would love to have the docks to themselves.
Wouldn't surprise me if they were in league with the pirates, somehow.
Not yet, no. But perhaps an intrepid person such as yourself could help with that?
They have a Dark Elf who oversees their operations. Suvaris Atheron.
She's meticulous to a fault, and keeps a logbook with every transaction she makes.
If it were to somehow end up in my hands, I wouldn't argue.
I know this place looks horrible -- please don't hold it against the Company.
Please excuse the mess.
If you get me the logbook, I'll have proof of Shatter-Shield's treachery.
Let me know when you've learned more about the pirates.
She's probably the one you ought to talk to.
We're so grateful for your help.
You think Alva... but that means...
Ye gods! You think Alva is a vampire?
I don't know much. Only the old tales about the ancient heroes who would use the Voice to defeat the enemies of Skyrim.
They say the emperors used to be Dragonborn, but that all ended when Martin died during the Oblivion Crisis.
The Greybeards can tell you much more than I can. They're the masters of the Way of the Voice and taught Tiber Septim himself.
The only thing I remember is the old story of Tiber Septim being called Dragonborn by the Nords, before they switched sides and joined the Empire.
No doubt those Greybeards up at High Hrothgar will talk your ear off about it.
They live in seclusion on the slopes of the Throat of the World, the great mountain of Skyrim.
They are masters of the Way of the Voice. If you really are Dragonborn, they're the ones who can teach you how to use your power.
Near as I can tell, a bunch of ancient Nord hermits who live on top of a mountain.
Never heard of them talking to anybody before. If they really do want to talk to you, you'd better go find out what they want. You never know.
In the old tales, Nord heroes would use the power of their Voice to Shout down the gates of cities and strike down their enemies.
But only the Greybeards study the Way of the Voice any more. If you can Shout... you need to talk to them.
Old Nord folk tales always talk about Shouting their enemies to pieces. I always thought it was just a myth.
If you think you might be able to do that kind of thing, the Legion could certainly use your help.
Go talk to the Greybeards, see what they can tell you.
I'm just the housecarl. If you need something, you should speak to Kraldar.
We may not have as much to offer as Whiterun or Solitude, but we'll do what we can to make your stay a pleasant one.
Winterhold doesn't offer much, but we like living here. Been here all our lives.
If you're here for the College, don't let the folks in town bother you. Just try and keep to yourself.
The mages at the College keep to themselves for the most part, so we don't see much of them.
Turns out who runs Winterhold doesn't change much. Can't say I'm surprised.
If you've business with the College, you're welcome to stay here. It's where most of our business comes from, in fact.
Unlike some folks here in town, we have nothing against the College or the people that come and go.
Don't worry about Nelacar. He has a permanent room here at the inn, but he keeps to himself and he doesn't cause any trouble.
Let me know if you need anything. We're here to provide.
Without us looking out for Winterhold, it's doomed. Simply doomed.
Korir will be the first to tell you that if it weren't for that college, we'd all be better off. I agree with him.
Winterhold might yet thrive if that college had never been built.
Splendid. Then we're ready to begin. Or, more specifically, you're ready to begin. After all, you're the one the Night Mother spoke to.
But by Sithis, I hope I'm making the right choice here. You may be an effective killer, but your command of guile leaves much to be desired.
The sealed letter you delivered to me was mysteriously unsealed upon its arrival. I can only assume you've read its contents. Sloppy...
But moving on to business. As you probably already know from reading the letter... you're going to a wedding.
Splendid. Then we're ready to begin. Or, more specifically, you're ready to begin. After all, you're the one the Night Mother spoke to.
Now, then. I hope you have something nice to wear. Because you're going to a wedding.
This is a public kill. How you do it is entirely up to you. Arrow to the throat? Knife in the belly? Your choice, so long as it's loud and messy.
Because of the current political climate, people are going to assume the murder is related to the bad blood between the Legion and Stormcloaks.
In any event, when Vici dies, it's going to be complete pandemonium. Best have your escape route planned out in advance.
Well, more like the public reception. It should be a lovely affair. You'll mingle with the guests, eat some cake... stab the bride.
Oh yes. You've got to kill the bride. At her wedding.
And they say romance is dead.
Hmph. He had a trip to Skyrim planned for months, and canceled at the last minute. Much to his cousin's dismay.
Seems the Emperor realized his presence would necessitate a more direct role in the ongoing hostilities. A role he was, obviously, unwilling to take.
All that will change with his cousin's murder. Everyone will think it was motivated by the conflict between the Imperial Legion and Stormcloaks.
And the Emperor will be forced to come clean up the mess.
If you need any help with Destruction spells, please let me know.
I'm sure when the time for promotions comes, they'll go to the most deserving mage.
Don't you think I'd make a wonderful master wizard? Maybe even Arch-Mage someday?
It's a small group of us here at the College. Favors don't go unremembered. Neither do affronts.
Another day, another opportunity to learn!
Someday the College may be fully accepted by the Nords. I hope that my work as Arch-Mage may help.
Like it or not, this college is a direct influence on the public's impressions of magic and those that use it.
It is up to each of us to present ourselves publicly as maturely as possible, and to remain above petty local squabbles.
A legendary bottle, forged in the days when Skyrim was just starting its turn to ice.
A small container, made of the magically infused snow that first fell on the Throat of the World.
It is said that the Phial will replenish whatever fluid is placed inside of it.
For an alchemist like myself, it is the most pure expression of my art. To create from nothingness is... poetry.
Bah. If there was a tonic that could help me, I would have found it by now...
I have spent my entire life searching for it.
It brought me all the way to these frozen reaches from the warm embrace of Summerset Isle.
Entire years spent in libraries. Seeking out tiny villages with local legends that contain but a whisper of a hint of the Phial.
I even named my shop after it, hoping that it might attract anyone who had heard of it.
And now it's within my grasp... but the Eight, it seems, have chosen me for their amusement.
You would do that? It's good to know there are some people out there who are willing to help an old man.
It's buried with its maker, Curalmil, in a long forsaken cave to the west of here.
Curalmil was a crafty one, even in death. You would need the skills of a master alchemist to reach its resting place.
Luckily for you, I've already made the mixture. Here, take it.
Please... don't dally. I've wasted enough time arguing with my useless assistant here.
May the Eight guide you to the Phial...
Please hurry -- I fear my master doesn't have much time left.
I assume you have proof? Can't go making accusations like that without proof.
Alva? Huh. Didn't think she had it in her.
So it's true. The traitorous bitch! Morthal owes you a debt.
Here. You were promised a reward for solving the crime, but I need one more favor from you.
Morthal is still in danger. The journal mentions Movarth, a master vampire I thought was destroyed a century ago.
I'll gather together some able-bodied warriors to clean out Movarth's Lair. They'll be waiting outside for you to lead them.
What? Were you hoping to claim this house for yourself? Well, the Aretinos always liked me, so I think I'm family enough to take up here.
The milk drinkers in this town wouldn't last five minutes in a real battle.
Rumor holds that that Aretino boy brought an assassin here. Sounds like nonsense to me.
I'm going with you whether you like it or not.
You want in there? Here's my spare key. Take whatever you want.
We were supposed to find a sacrifice. Human sacrifice. I told her I couldn't do it and she attacked.
I've done horrible things living here, but never taken an innocent life. I couldn't just find some person for them to kill.
The Initiation. My mother is going to become one of those things. I can't be a part of that.
Gods, I can't run away from this. I have to stop her.
Let's get up there before Mother finds out what we're doing.
Nasty little buggers, aren't they?
The mine will be running better than ever thanks to you.
Sorry, the mine's dry right now.
I hate seeing a good mine go to waste like that, you know?
If you're looking for supplies, I'd advise heading to Riften.
You think you're tough, huh? Wandering Skyrim is a picnic compared to tunnel crawling!
The real work happens in places like this. Blood and sweat is what makes the world turn.
Sure, I used to be a soldier... but that was child's play compared to cracking rock.
Came all the way out here to see us, did you?
Making that trip to Darkwater Crossing was a huge help. Thank you.
I trust my parents are well?
Aye, I see the look you're giving me. Don't worry, I won't bite.
You're looking at the best miner in Skyrim.
I'd wager I could crack rock faster than anyone. Even you.
Ha! You showed those spiders a thing or two!
We're married. I guess we'll need to decide where we're living, won't we? You can always stay with me.
I'm hoping to run my own inn someday.
Please stop looking at me like that.
First the war and now there's talk of dragons. What's next?
You mentioned something like that... when you were running off with my goat!
Tell you what - you bring back my goat, and maybe I'll give a damn about your staff.
You said a whole lot of nonsense about selling Gleda to hire a priestess for some big event.
There was a bit of talk about some staff you had in safe keeping. Never said where, but you sure didn't have it with you!
It's good to see a fresh face. You wouldn't have happen to come from Whiterun would you?
If you're looking for potions you should talk to my aunt, Angeline Morrard.
Hmph. You know, you've got a certain... fire in your eyes. Just like my Beirand. Please, feel free to look around. We've got a bit of everything.
Hey! Wanna play tag?
I bet my papa could beat you up.
I've got weapons for givin', and armor for gettin'!
Seems there's no end to the needs of the Imperials. But what can I help you with?
Why, hello! Here to buy? If you have to ask the price, you might be in the wrong store.
We provide clothing for all of Solitude's most... influential residents. But for you, I'll make an exception.
Oh, what delight. Another charming customer...
Oh, let me just drop everything to help you. I don't mind. Really.
Welcome to the Winking Skeever, friend.
If you need a good rumor or two, I think I can oblige...
Traitors, civil wars, dragons. Bah. Those troubles don't cross my doorway. So sit, relax.
Don't mind me. I'm just running errands for the old man.
Papa says I won't see Roggvir anymore, but he wouldn't tell me why not.
Spare some gold for a veteran?
Plenty of fresh fish for ya.
Pardon me.
I don't have anything to say.
Welcome to Solitude. Home of the arts here in Skyrim.
I'm Pantea Ateia, the master vocalist.
Hello, yes? I'm the Dean of History. And scribe work, when I can find some quiet time.
I wish only to graduate and enroll with the Legion. My drums will lead our troops to victory.
Lady Ateia says my voice is second only to hers. But she's selling me short.
Dean Inge says a bard can never have enough practice. So, would you like a tune?
Unless we have specific business, I'm not interested.
The Imperials are good for business, and business is good for Skyrim.
Welcome to the center of the world. Or at least my brother Erikur's world...
Speak if you must, but mind your tongue. I do not suffer fools gladly.
Mmm... Spiced Wine for ya? It's a family recipe.
I'm too old to work, but that don't make me too old to drink.
Hmm? Sorry, I was lost in thought. I'm planning my wedding, you see. So many details to work out.
You look like you could do with a ripe apple. Or a tomato, cabbage, or leek?
Not much work being a jailor. Long as you can ignore the pleading and all that.
With the passing of my dear husband Torygg, the weighty position of jarl has fallen to me. Please direct questions to my steward.
I am the jarl's steward. If you have court business to tend to, you talk to me.
Please address all questions to the steward, Falk Firebeard.
Just arrive in the city? Is it true what they say, then? There are dragons about?
Be quick. I have little patience for mundane concerns.
Jarls come and jarls go, but a good cook stays for a lifetime. And I'm a very, very good cook.
Oh, hello! Just tidying up.
Watch your feet. We just cleaned there!
Hello, my child. Arkay watch over you.
Blessings of the Eight Divines upon you. How may I help?
Blessings to you. The Divines are listening.
I have no business with you. Leave me.
You, talk to egg-brothers. Deeja has no words for you.
Dock work is not good work. Better than Black Marsh, though. Much better.
Oh my, old Ma'zaka has a visitor!
Ah, yet another new face. I'm from Hammerfell, you see. I can hardly keep track of all the people I've been meeting.
Make it quick. I get enough delays from politicians.
Something to report?
You here for my next interrogation? I got nothing but time...
You got business at the stables?
Is this about the stables? My husband, Geimund, handles the business.
Looking for a horse? You'll want to talk to my father, Geimund.
What do you want?
My duty is simple - oversee the Thalmor Justiciars, and make sure they perform their duties admirably.
Can I help you?
Oh, hello. Have a seat, will you?
